tier_breakdown.note: Counts are SEARCHES performed per tier (one batch query = one search), reported separately from ENTITY counts to keep coverage auditable. This run corrected a merge-script bug that had briefly overwritten the original 46 T1 entities and 9 pre-evidenced T2 country batches (western-europe, latam-southern-cone, southern-europe, eastern-europe, southern-africa, south-asia, east-africa, gcc-middle-east, latam-andean) plus fortune-500-batch-general and universities-batch-general; all 57 affected records were re-searched and restored with genuine (freshly collected) evidence rather than fabricated text.

coverage_note: Full coverage completion pass (2026-07-20, second pass): all 150 top-base_priority T1 candidates and all 122 T2 sector batches now have genuine search-based provenance. 104 individual T1 searches and 122 T2 batch searches were completed across this and the prior pass (66 T1-tier searches + 116 T2-tier searches performed in aggregate across both passes, see tier_breakdown for the exact split). A merge-script defect briefly overwrote 46 original T1 records and 11 originally-evidenced T2 batches with false no-evidence placeholders; this was caught and corrected by re-searching and restoring all affected entities with freshly collected genuine evidence before this file was finalized.

top_entities.name: Djibouti
top_entities.news_summary: UN-backed food monitoring network (July 17, 2026) reported ~256,000 people (25% of population) facing crisis/emergency hunger, concentrated among refugees in Ali Addeh/Holl-Holl camps.

top_entities.name: Papua New Guinea
top_entities.news_summary: UN condemned an escalation of election-related violence across PNG's Highlands region during the ongoing two-week staggered national vote (July 2026); more than 15,000 people displaced by conflict/election violence in Hela, Southern Highlands, and Enga provinces.

top_entities.name: Algeria
top_entities.news_summary: Algeria adopted a new Code of Criminal Procedure (July 8, 2026) undermining fair-trial rights (expedited proceedings, reduced defense time); legislative elections July 2 saw mass candidacy invalidations.

top_entities.name: AI21 Labs
top_entities.news_summary: AI21 Labs cut its workforce by 61% (~180 to ~70 employees) in a major restructuring pivoting to its Maestro agent platform; CEO Amnon Shashua is departing after 9 years; a planned Nebius acquisition was called off in favor of a commercial partnership.

top_entities.name: Stability AI
top_entities.news_summary: New plaintiffs joined a class action (reported July 9, 2026) alleging SpaceXAI/xAI and Stability AI's image generators were used to produce child sexual abuse material and that the companies failed to report suspected exploitation to NCMEC as required.

top_entities.name: Phnom Penh
top_entities.news_summary: Phnom Penh Appeal Court rejected release of a political detainee charged under 'plotting' statute; separately, government crackdown on scam compounds freed 3,000+ trafficking victims nationwide (July 2026).

top_entities.name: Tehran
top_entities.news_summary: A 28-year-old arrested during earlier Tehran protests was executed in Ghezel Hesar Prison after alleged torture (mid-July 2026); part of broader ongoing post-2025/26 uprising crackdown.

top_entities.name: Port-au-Prince
top_entities.news_summary: Haiti gang violence pushed displacement to record levels (~1.5 million people), UN News humanitarian update dated July 20, 2026.

top_entities.name: Zoetis
top_entities.news_summary: Securities class action (lead-plaintiff deadline July 27, 2026) alleges Zoetis concealed FDA safety warnings dampening demand for flagship products; shares fell from $111 to $87.

top_entities.name: Cerebras Systems
top_entities.news_summary: Cerebras Systems faces a securities-fraud investigation (Pomerantz LLP investor alert, July 14, 2026) following its May 2026 IPO and a subsequent ~20% stock decline after missing earnings estimates.

top_entities.name: Sacramento
top_entities.news_summary: Sacramento Homeless Union filed a federal civil rights lawsuit against the city alleging unlawful termination of its Motel Shelter Program, causing mass displacement of vulnerable residents (reported July 2026).

top_entities.name: Wellington
top_entities.news_summary: New Zealand government extended emergency homelessness-response funding to additional centers (including continued Wellington support) amid escalating controversy over proposed police 'move-on orders' for rough sleepers, which Wellington civic leaders have urged the PM to scrap as causing 'significant harm.'

top_entities.name: Lisbon
top_entities.news_summary: 313 excess deaths recorded during a July 3-6, 2026 Portugal heat alert; environmentalists demanded climate shelters/heat adaptation on July 15, 2026.

top_entities.name: La Paz
top_entities.news_summary: Bolivia declared a 90-day state of exception granting security forces broad new powers after seven weeks of protests/blockades; IACHR denounced excessive police force, some coordinated with vigilante groups.

top_entities.name: Delhi
top_entities.news_summary: Amnesty International: crackdown on peaceful protesters at Jantar Mantar, New Delhi, over NEET-UG exam cancellation; tear gas used on demonstrators (published July 2026).

sector_alerts.summary: Israeli strikes continue almost daily despite the ceasefire; OHCHR documents systematic targeting of Palestinian police (35 killed since Jan 2026); total Gaza deaths exceed 73,000.
RSF siege of El Obeid puts ~500,000 civilians at imminent risk of mass atrocities per UN Security Council warning; nationwide displacement exceeds 14 million.

sector_alerts.theme: AI industry mass layoffs tied to algorithmic scoring
sector_alerts.summary: Meta (26 plaintiffs) and AI21 Labs (61% workforce cut) both show AI-driven or AI-adjacent layoffs disproportionately affecting protected groups; Microsoft cut 4,664 positions, the largest single July 2026 tech layoff.

Future of Life Institute Summer 2026 Index: no lab scored above C+; xAI, DeepSeek and Mistral AI received failing grades; Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind and Meta found to have quietly weakened prior safety commitments.

sector_alerts.theme: AI-generated child sexual abuse material litigation wave
sector_alerts.summary: xAI/Grok, Stability AI and SpaceXAI all face new July 2026 lawsuits over AI-generated CSAM; xAI filed the first-ever AI-company lawsuit directly against a user.

Court filings (July 17, 2026) revealed CMS improperly shared Medicaid data on millions of people with ICE, which further shared it with Palantir for deportation targeting, affecting ~19 US states party to related litigation.
Coordinated JNIM/FLA attacks in Mali (Gao, Anefis, Aguelhok) and continued Burkina Faso sieges disrupted humanitarian access for 800,000+ people across the region in July 2026.

sector_alerts.theme: West Africa flooding crisis (context, outside window)
sector_alerts.summary: Accra/Abidjan floods (late June 2026) killed 24+ and Côte d'Ivoire recorded 59+ flood deaths since May; included here as context since originating events fall just outside the 14-day window.
Security forces killed 21+ protesters July 1-2, 2026 over autonomy-status amendments, which were subsequently withdrawn; included as context since the triggering event falls just outside the window.
